leadText: Hydro Pump (Japanese: ハイドロポンプ Hydro Pump) is a damage-dealing Water-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TR03 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM142 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM096 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I to V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Hydro Pump inflicts damage and has no secondary effect. Generation I to V [ edit | edit source ] Hydro Pump's power is 120. Hydro Pump can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Rain Dance was used in the previous turn. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Hydro Pump's power changed from 120 to 110. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 5 Agile Strong Power 100 80 120 Accuracy 85% 85% 95% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect ? ? ? ? ? ? None Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] When the move is used by Blastoise , two smaller streams of water are fired instead of one large stream. Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 110 132 Cooldown 12s Move wind-up time 50 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

The target is blasted by a huge volume of water launched under great pressure. MD GtI It damages even a faraway Pokémon. It damages even faraway Pokémon. * Super Smash Bros. for Nintendo 3DS/Wii U Two bursts while airborne; (joystick) selects the direction. * S MD It even damages faraway Pokémon. Super Smash Bros. Ultimate Fires a powerful water jet, propelling itself in whichever direction the stick is tilted. * MD RT DX It fires off water with incredible force to damage even faraway Pokémon. Payment from the bank to made to the owner of the location.)

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The move's name is incorrectly formatted as Hydropump in several Kids' WB! promos. In 3D games before Pokémon X and Y , when Blastoise uses this move, two streams are fired from its cannons, whereas every other Pokémon only has one stream. Starting in Pokémon X and Y, Blastoise no longer had a unique animation for this move. However, the animation was brought back when Blastoise returned in The Indigo Disk . Since Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! , the move has the same sound effect as it originally did in Generation I. The way Hydro Pump is portrayed when used by Ash's Squirtle in Pokémon the Series is a reference to Gamera 's spinning flight. This motion was also utilized for Blastoise 's Rapid Spin in Pokémon UNITE and Pokkén Tournament DX .
